sglang.title: fix multimodal hash feature
sglang.author: huangtingwei9988
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
When a hash calculation is performed on a tensor, it is usually calculated based on the tensor's address. When two tensors have the same value but different addresses, their hash values ​​are different.
# example

import torch

randn_tensor = torch.randn(4, 1024)
print(hash(randn_tensor))

vllm.author: WangErXiao
vllm.bodyExcerpt: Update image to latest version in deployment docs

vllm.title: Upstream Llama4 Support to Main
vllm.author: houseroad
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
As a follow up of #16104, we upstream the Llama4 support to the main branch.
The goal of this PR:

Support the llama4
Clean up some hacks

More enhancement will be tracked in #16114.
Fixes from v0.8.3:

Fix missing sampler
Fix failing CI on transformers==4.51.0

FIX #16177

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Re-enable support for `ChatGLMForConditionalGeneration`
vllm.author: DarkLight1337
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
ChatGLM3 originally had ChatGLMForConditionalGeneration architecture before being changed to ChatGLMModels. As a result, models derived from the original version may still use this architecture. This PR re-enables support for those models.
FIX #16183

vllm.title: [V1] Revert the default `max_num_seqs` to V0 values for most hardware
vllm.author: DarkLight1337
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR reverts the default max_num_seqs from 1024 to 256 for hardware other than H100/200 since many people have run into OOM when using V1.
Note: This does not solve #15664 which is about OOM even when using the same max_num_seqs as V0.

vllm.title: [Misc] Print encoder seq len to short warning only once
vllm.author: gshtras

vllm.title: [Misc] Human-readable `max-model-len` cli arg
vllm.author: NickLucche
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
--max--model-len 10000000 ==> --max--model-len 10M
Supports k/m/g/K/M/G in human-readable format. Including decimal values with decimal multipliers.
Examples:

'1k' -> 1,000
'1K' -> 1,024
'25.6k' -> 25,600

vllm.title: [Misc] Move Llama 4 projector call into encoder execution
vllm.author: ywang96
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
On vLLM we consider encoder execution to generate multimodal embeddings ready to be merged into text embeddings, thus it often includes the projector call even though it's not part of encoder. This PR moves the projection call into the encoder execution (i.e, get_multimodal_embeddings)
Tested with p

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Fix guidance backend for Qwen models
vllm.author: benchislett
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
The smaller of the Qwen models (such as Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-3B-Instruct for instance) have padding on their vocab size, such that the tokenizer will report a vocab_size which is smaller than the logits by 256 or 128.
This is an error for llguidance, which generates masks of the smaller size and there

vllm.author: njhill
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Problem introduced by #15906
Also change polling info message to debug.

vllm.title: [Bugfix][V0] XGrammar structured output supports Enum
vllm.author: leon-seidel
vllm.bodyExcerpt: XGrammar Enums support was added for V1 ( #15594) but not for V0 yet. This PR mirrors the fix and the test from their V1 counterpart.

vllm.title: Torchao
vllm.author: drisspg
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Summary
Added TorchAO quantization support to vLLM.
The required dependencies have been merged:

 [AO PR](pytorch/ao#1875)
 [HF PR](huggingface/transformers#36526)

Some discussion points

I added a mechanism for doing on the fly quant via overriding the hf quant config. I am okay with removing this

vllm.title: [ROCm][Bugfix][FP8] Make fp8 quant respect fused modules mapping
vllm.author: mgoin
vllm.bodyExcerpt: I thought this would be a better fix for the issue given in #15900

vllm.title: [core] do not send error across process
vllm.author: youkaichao
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
there are errors reported from #15592 and #16009 , caused by passing errors across processes.
this pr changes the error handling logic, we log the error in the worker side, and only send the message to the main process.

vllm.title: [Misc] Update compressed-tensors to version 0.9.3
vllm.author: mlsw
vllm.bodyExcerpt: The current version of llm-compressor (0.5.0) is incompatible with vLLM. This is because it requires compressed-tensors==0.9.3, while vLLM is pinned to compressed-tensors==0.9.2. This PR updates the version of compressed-tensors pinned by vLLM.

vllm.title: Update BASE_IMAGE to 2.22 release of Neuron
vllm.author: aws-satyajith
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Update BASE_IMAGE to 2.22 release of Neuron in docker/Dockerfile.neuron
Add tenacity dependency
Related to RFC #15970

Local test run result:
2 passed, 1 warning in 65.82s (0:01:05)

This PR is an attempt to move scatter_patch_features and gather_patch_feature into the model runner (outside of the model) to avoid interfering with TPU graph compilation.
Breaking change for model developers:

PromptUpdateDetails.features has been replaced w

Same fix as #16112 to add use_irope flag to attention backends that might be used in Llama4. Didn't make changes for MLA backends which are not used in Llama4
Fixes #16189
Tested linter happy with pre-commit run --show-diff-on-failure --color=always --hook-stage manual --all-files

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Do not skip "empty" parts of chats that are parsable
vllm.author: mgoin
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
We identified an issue with passing in empty system messages to Mistral3 i.e. having {"role": "system", "content": ""} in your chat completion messages. There are two issues: one with the chat template and one with vLLM.
The gist for this audience is now we send a properly non-empty list to apply_ch

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Fix and reorganize broken GGUF tests and bump gguf version
vllm.author: Isotr0py
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
FIX #16180
FIX #16134

The GGUF support is broken with numpy 2.0 installation, because we hardcode at a very old gguf version, this PR bump the gguf version to keep compatability with numpy 2.0.
This PR also reorganizes the GGUF tests to keep Llama gguf test running on standard test.
Also fix the GG

vllm.title: [torch.compile][TPU] Make @support_torch_compile work for XLA backend
vllm.author: lsy323
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Make @support_torch_compile work for XLA backend. With the custom dispatcher, overhead of dynamo guard evaluation is eliminated.
For TPU backend, each models have 2 FX graphs/dynamo bytecodes:

During profiling run - No KV cache
After profiling run - with KV cache

This breaks the assumption in the

vllm.title: [V1] Add `disable_chunked_mm_input` arg to disable partial mm input prefill
vllm.author: mgoin
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Introduces a disable_chunked_mm_input argument to SchedulerConfig that can prevent partial scheduling of tokens from a multimodal input item, used in V1. If the scheduled range would only cover part of the mm input, roll back to only schedule the tokens before the mm item.
This ensures that if a req
